title: Arena Allocator Block Chaining Patterns
description: Research arena/bump allocator patterns from Rust bumpalo, Zig ArenaAllocator, and C++ implementations, focusing on multi-block chaining strategies, reset with memory reuse, and type-safe API design for Aria Phase 4.2.

scope.arena_fundamentals: Arena/region/bump allocator core concepts and use cases
scope.block_chaining: Multi-block arena growth strategies (fixed-size, exponential, hybrid)
scope.memory_reuse: Arena reset() patterns that preserve allocated blocks for reuse
scope.type_safety: Type-safe arena API design leveraging language features

rust_bumpalo: How does bumpalo implement multi-block chaining (overflow to new chunks)?
What is bumpalo's block allocation strategy (initial size, growth policy)?
How does bumpalo handle alignment for mixed-type allocations?
What is bumpalo's reset() behavior (memory reuse vs deallocation)?
How does bumpalo integrate with Rust's type system (lifetime safety)?
What zero-cost abstractions does bumpalo provide?
How does bumpalo handle allocations larger than chunk size?
What performance characteristics does bumpalo achieve (ns per allocation)?
zig_arena: How does Zig's ArenaAllocator implement block chaining?
What backing allocator pattern does Zig use (delegation to std.heap.page_allocator)?
How does Zig track multiple blocks (linked list, array)?
What is Zig's deinit() behavior (bulk deallocation of all blocks)?
How does Zig's allocator interface work (std.mem.Allocator trait)?
What alignment guarantees does Zig's arena provide?
How does Zig handle allocation failures (error unions)?
What reset() or reuse mechanisms exist in Zig arenas?
cpp_patterns: What C++ arena allocator patterns exist (single-block, linked-list, pool-based)?
How do C++ arenas handle alignment (alignof, align_to)?
What RAII patterns exist for arena cleanup (unique_ptr, defer-like)?
How do modern C++ arenas integrate with STL allocators?
How do C++ arenas handle large allocations (>chunk size)?
What memory mapping strategies exist (mmap, VirtualAlloc, huge pages)?
What benchmarks exist comparing arena vs malloc performance?
aria_integration: How should Aria's Arena.alloc<T>() provide type safety?
How can Aria's defer integrate with arena cleanup?
What API should Arena.reset() provide (zero-copy reuse)?
How should Arena handle @alignof(T) for mixed types?
What statistics API should Arena provide (bytes_allocated, bytes_reserved)?
How should Arena handle allocations > block_size (error vs oversized block)?
What validation mechanisms should Arena provide (magic numbers)?
How should Arena integrate with wild pointer semantics (not GC-tracked)?

deliverables: Arena allocator fundamentals and use cases (300-400 lines)
Rust bumpalo architecture analysis with code examples
Zig ArenaAllocator architecture analysis with code examples
Type-safe API design recommendations for Aria
Performance benchmarks and targets (ns per allocation)
Integration with Aria's defer and wild pointers
Code examples in Rust, Zig, C++, and proposed Aria syntax

existing_baseline.stabilized_bump: 1.6-45ns allocation, pure bump (no chaining), single pool
existing_baseline.segfit_segregated: 56ns allocation, 15 size classes, doubly-linked free lists
existing_baseline.wild_alloc: ~100ns allocation, TBB sticky errors, individual free()

performance_targets.reset_speed: O(1) time to reset arena for reuse
